articles.title: Vail Resorts Profits Fall as Poor Snowfall Puts Damper on 2025-26 Season
articles.description: Vail Resorts reported its quarterly earnings for Q2 of the 2026 fiscal year ended January 31. According to the press release and 10Q filing from March 9, the company has been struggling with the historically low snowfall affecting many of its key resorts, resulting in lower quarterly earnings and lower visitation numbers. Chief executive Rob Katz described the winter as […] The post Vail Resorts Profits Fall as Poor Snowfall Puts Damper on 2025-26 Season appeared first on SnowBrains .
articles.content: Vail Resorts, which now owns more than 40 ski areas worldwide, revolutionized the business of skiing. | Image: Vail Resorts Vail Resorts reported its quarterly earnings for Q2 of the 2026 fiscal year ended January 31. According to the press release and 10Q filing from March 9, the company has been struggling with the historically low snowfall affecting many of its key resorts, resulting in lower quarterly earnings and lower visitation numbers. Chief executive Rob Katz described the winter as “the most challenging across the Rockies that we have ever experienced,” citing the lowest snowfall in more than 30 years at resorts in Colorado and Utah. Limited terrain and warm temperatures reduced visits and spending across the company’s destination resorts. Robert Katz returned as CEO in May last year in a bid to boost the company’s stagnating business. Q2 resort reported EBITDA was down 8.3 percent year-over-year and net income was off 15 percent, while season-to-date skier visits as of March 1 were down nearly 12 percent compared to the same period last

articles.title: Vail Resorts Reports Declining Skier Visits — What the Numbers Reveal About the Modern Ski Industry
articles.description: Vail Resorts has reported a significant drop in skier visits during the 2025–26 winter season, highlighting how weather variability and changing travel patterns can affect ski tourism. Yet the company’s pass-based model has helped stabilize revenue despite fewer visitors on the slopes. This article examines what the numbers reveal about the Epic Pass system, resort economics and the broader shifts shaping the global ski industry. The post Vail Resorts Reports Declining Skier Visits — What the Nu
articles.content: Early season operating metrics released by Vail Resorts show a noticeable decline in skier visits across its North American resort portfolio during the 2025–26 winter season. The figures, reported through the company’s investor communications and widely covered by financial and industry media, offer an interesting window into how the modern ski resort business model operates during a difficult winter . While the headline numbers point to a challenging season, the underlying data reveals something more nuanced about how large resort operators manage financial volatility. Skier Visits Down — One of the Sharpest Drops in Recent Years According to season-to-date operating metrics, skier visits across Vail Resorts’ North American mountain resorts were down approximately 20% compared with the previous season . The company attributes the decline primarily to weak early-season snowfall across the Western United States , which significantly delayed terrain openings at several major destinations

articles.title: Vail Resorts Admits Visits & Revenue Down
articles.description: As expected, numbers have fallen after a poor winter for snow in the many of the major US resorts. NEW The post Vail Resorts Admits Visits & Revenue Down appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: The US resort owner said it is due to a ‘worst-case weather scenario’. The comments come from the CEO, Rob Katz, and follows one of the worst winters for snow in decades in parts of the USA. In a news release for shareholders and investors the company admitted: Skier visits fell 11.9% Total lift revenue down 3.6% Ski school revenue down 8.2% Dining revenue down 8.6% Retail/rental revenue down 5.7% In the second financial quarter the net revenue decreased 4.7% , that’s $53.2m. “This has been the most challenging winter across the Rockies that we have ever experienced with the lowest snowfall levels in more than 30 years for our Colorado and Utah resorts, combined with warmer temperatures, resulting in reduced terrain throughout the quarter and into February,” said Katz in a statement. Vail Resorts’ stock price fell more than 3% after the earnings announcement. Skiing in the USA. Image © PlanetSKI. Vail Resorts said it was pleased with the strength and stability shown by the operating mo

articles.title: Wintersteiger V‑Edge: Better Tuning for Skis + New Revenue Ops for Ski Retailers
articles.description: Wintersteiger V‑Edge: Versatile Tuning for Every SkierProvides New Revenue Opportunities for Ski Industry Retailers Salt Lake City, UT (February 26, 2026) — Wintersteiger has renewed its emphasis on V-Edge, the variable‑edge grinding function built into all Jupiter automated ski service machines, providing retailers with high‑margin services that improve turn and exit initiation for skiers of […] The post Wintersteiger V‑Edge: Better Tuning for Skis + New Revenue Ops for Ski Retailers appeared f
articles.content: Wintersteiger V‑Edge: Versatile Tuning for Every Skier Provides New Revenue Opportunities for Ski Industry Retailers Salt Lake City, UT (February 26, 2026) — Wintersteiger has renewed its emphasis on V-Edge, the variable‑edge grinding function built into all Jupiter automated ski service machines, providing retailers with high‑margin services that improve turn and exit initiation for skiers of all disciplines. “V-Edge gives shops a simple, reliable way to deliver a noticeable customer benefit and drive incremental revenue from the equipment they already have,” said Wes Price, Sales Manager at Wintersteiger. “It’s easy to train staff on the Comfort, Sport, and Race settings, and the consistent results help shops convert more rentals, increase repeat business, and strengthen guest loyalty.” Key benefits for ski shops: Jupiter owners can offer V-Edge tuning as an upsell for rental fleets, demo skis, retail, and race customers, without additional major hardware purchases. Three pre-program

articles.title: Pila Records 12,000 Skiers in One Day as Season Surges
articles.description: Pila ski resort records 12,000 visitors in one day during Carnival weekend, with season entries up 9% and revenue up 16% as weather disruptions affect neighbouring resorts. The post Pila Records 12,000 Skiers in One Day as Season Surges appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: Pila Records a Historic Ski Day The ski resort of Pila in the Aosta Valley has recorded a historic peak of 12,000 skier visits in a single day over the Carnival weekend, confirming what is shaping up to be one of the strongest winter seasons in recent years. Early morning queues formed at the Aosta–Pila gondola base station as visitors arrived in large numbers. Despite the crowds, slope conditions remained good throughout the day thanks to solid snow cover and efficient lift operations. Planning a trip to Pila? For detailed piste maps, where to stay, lift access tips and insider advice, read my full Must-Read Guide to Pila Ski Resort . Season Performance: Growth Across the Board With roughly two months remaining before the scheduled May 3 closure, Pila’s winter 2025/26 figures are already ahead of last year: +9% increase in first entries +16% increase in revenue More than 400,000 skier entries recorded so far Over €14 million in turnover The numbers confirm a season that is not only bu

articles.title: Transformative Big Sky Leader Taylor Middleton Dies at 68
articles.description: SAM Magazine—Big Sky, Mont., Feb. 23, 2026—Taylor Middleton, the longtime Big Sky Resort executive who led the Montana destination through decades of expansion and repositioned it as one of North America’s premier ski resorts, died Friday at his home in Big Sky. He was 68. An Alabama native and 1979 University of Alabama graduate, Middleton arrived at Big Sky 46 years ago as a front desk attendant at the Huntley Lodge. He moved into sales and marketing, became general manager in 1998, and later

articles.title: Ikon Pass Owner Alterra Mountain Company CEO to Step Down at End of Ski Season
articles.description: Alterra Mountain Company CEO Jared Smith will step down at the end of the ski season, marking a leadership transition at the company behind the Ikon Pass. The change comes as global multi-resort passes and corporate consolidation continue reshaping the ski industry and competition between major resort operators. The post Ikon Pass Owner Alterra Mountain Company CEO to Step Down at End of Ski Season appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: A leadership change is coming to one of the most influential companies in the global ski industry. Jared Smith has announced he will step down as chief executive officer of Alterra Mountain Company at the end of the current ski season, marking a transition at the company behind the Ikon Pass. The Denver-based resort operator said Smith will remain involved during the transition while the company searches for a new chief executive. A Leadership Change at One of the Ski Industry’s Largest Operators Alterra Mountain Company is one of the two dominant corporate players in modern ski tourism, alongside Vail Resorts. Founded in 2018 and headquartered in Denver, Alterra operates and partners with dozens of ski destinations across North America and internationally. The company’s Ikon Pass provides access to more than 70 ski areas worldwide through a mix of owned resorts and partner agreements. Under Smith’s leadership, Alterra continued expanding both its resort portfolio and its global pass n

articles.title: Indy Pass Owner Moves Headquarters to New Hampshire as Black Mountain Becomes Innovation Hub
articles.description: Indy Pass owner Erik Mogensen is relocating headquarters to New Hampshire while turning Black Mountain into an innovation hub designed to help independent ski areas compete in a consolidating ski industry. The post Indy Pass Owner Moves Headquarters to New Hampshire as Black Mountain Becomes Innovation Hub appeared first on The-Ski-Guru .
articles.content: The company behind the rapidly expanding Indy Pass has announced it will relocate its headquarters to Jackson, New Hampshire, while transforming the historic Black Mountain ski area into what it calls an “Independent Mountain Laboratory.” Erik Mogensen, CEO of Entabeni Systems , said the mountain will serve as a real-world testing ground for technologies, operational systems and management strategies designed to help independent ski resorts compete in an industry increasingly dominated by large corporate operators. The announcement includes the buyout of early investors in Black Mountain, returning the resort to 100 percent private ownership under Mogensen’s leadership . A Test Mountain for Independent Ski Areas Black Mountain will operate both as a ski area and as an innovation hub where new software, hardware and operational tools can be developed and tested before being shared with other independent ski resorts. According to Mogensen, many smaller ski areas lack the resources and ti

articles.title: Ski Industry Giants Shake Up Continues: Alterra CEO Jared Smith to Step Down After 2025-26 Season
articles.description: Alterra Mountain Company announced a leadership transition Tuesday, March 10, with Chief Executive Officer Jared Smith planning to step down at the end of the current winter season. Smith said serving as CEO had been “an honor,” noting that the company had expanded significantly during his tenure. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply […] The post Ski Industry Giants Shake Up Continues: Alterra CEO Jared Smith to Step Down After 2025-26 Season appeared first on SnowBrains
articles.content: Jared Smith served as CEO at Alterra since June 2022. | Image: Alterra Mountain Company Alterra Mountain Company announced a leadership transition Tuesday, March 10, with Chief Executive Officer Jared Smith planning to step down at the end of the current winter season. Smith said serving as CEO had been “an honor,” noting that the company had expanded significantly during his tenure. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply proud of the incredible people, capabilities and businesses we’ve added to this amazing company,” Smith said in the company’s press release. “The commitment of the ownership group to these mountains, and to the team members and communities they serve, is truly unique.” Alterra’s board will now begin a comprehensive search for a new chief executive, while an interim leadership structure will oversee operations through the end of the season. Smith will remain available over the next year to help support the transition. During the transition, an Ex

articles.title: Alterra Mountain Company Announces Departure of CEO
articles.description: During his tenure, Smith continued the growth of Alterra, which included acquisitions like Arapahoe Basin, Schweitzer, and Ski Butlers.
articles.content: Denver, Colorado — In a surprising development, one of the big players in North America’s ski industry is changing up its leadership team. Yesterday, the Alterra Mountain Company announced that Jared Smith will be leaving his position at the end of the 2025-26 ski season. A reason why was not given. “Serving as CEO of Alterra has been an honor, and I am deeply proud of the incredible people, capabilities and businesses we’ve added to this amazing company, ” said Jared Smith. “The commitment of the ownership group to these mountains, and to the team members and communities they serve, is truly unique. I’m confident the progress we’ve made has positioned the company well to build on that foundation in the years of growth ahead.” Jared Smith was first hired as the company’s President in 2021 and then became CEO in August 2022. He’s a bit of an enigma, which is the benefit you have when you’re running a private company. He conducted some interviews during his tenure, including a couple wit

articles.title: Smith to Step Down as Alterra CEO
articles.description: SAM Magazine—Denver, March 11, 2026— Jared Smith plans to step down from his role as chief executive officer of Alterra Mountain Company at the end of this season. An exact end date was not shared. Smith joined Alterra as president in June 2021, reporting to then-CEO Rusty Gregory. He previously served as president and global chairman of Ticketmaster. He succeeded Gregory as CEO on Aug. 1, 2022. During his time at Alterra, the company grew its portfolio of ski areas as well as related businesses

articles.title: French Ski Resorts Invest €555 Million in 2025, Maintaining Record-Level Commitment
articles.description: French ski resorts invested €555 million in 2025, maintaining a historically high level of spending and reinforcing the sector’s central role in mountain economies, according to the annual investment survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. The total is nearly identical to 2024 and stands 50% above the ten-year average, keeping […] The post French Ski Resorts Invest €555 Million in 2025, Maintaining Record-Level Commitment ap
articles.content: Ski resorts across France including Tignes (pictured) received €555 million worth of investments in 2025. | Image: SnowBrains French ski resorts invested €555 million in 2025, maintaining a historically high level of spending and reinforcing the sector’s central role in mountain economies, according to the annual investment survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. The total is nearly identical to 2024 and stands 50% above the ten-year average, keeping investment near record levels since the barometer was created. According to the survey, investment in 2025 represents 32% of pre-tax turnover—nine percentage points above the 10-year average—demonstrating how large a share of revenue operators are directing back into infrastructure and modernization. Related: Les Arcs, France, Report: Last Run of the Trip… 22 Powder Days in a Row Here! Industry representatives say sustaining that level of commitment has become more com

articles.title: French Ski Resorts Invest for the Future
articles.description: French ski resorts invested €555m in 2025, confirming the key role of investment in mountain regions. Much is at high altitude and main resorts see a bright future ahead. NEW The post French Ski Resorts Invest for the Future appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: In 2025, French ski resorts committed €555m (£486)in investments, according to the annual survey conducted by Montagne Leaders magazine in partnership with Atout France and Domaines Skiables de France. This amount is identical to the previous year and is 50% above the ten-year average. It keeps investment at a record level since the barometer was created. In terms of turnover, the investment represents 32% of pre-tax turnover, which is 9 points above the ten-year average, confirming a particularly high level of financial commitment. The breakdown of investments for 2025 by mountain range, excluding grooming, is €230.26m (£201.8m) in Savoie €84m (£73.5m) in Haute-Savoie €80.16m (£70.2m) in Isère €71.84m (£63m)in the Southern Alps €34.9m (£30.5m) in the Pyrenees, €0.88m (£0.77m)in the Vosges €1.17 m (£1.02m)in the Jura €5.61m (£4.9m)in the Massif Central Sustained effort in a context of increased constraints Long-term analysis shows that ski areas must devote an increasingly large share

articles.title: Mad River Glen, VT, Buys 1,100 Acres to Conserve Land for Backcountry Use
articles.description: Mad River Glen has finalized the purchase of more than 1,100 acres of land surrounding its ski area in Fayston, Vermont, securing the property for conservation and backcountry recreation. The cooperatively owned ski area closed the $2.5 million deal Tuesday, general manager Matt Lillard told VTDigger. The acquisition more than doubles the land owned by the resort’s cooperative, which previously […] The post Mad River Glen, VT, Buys 1,100 Acres to Conserve Land for Backcountry Use appeared first
articles.content: Mad River Glen has purchased a surrounding 1,100 acres of land for recreational and conservation purposes. Photo: Mad River Glen’s iconic single chair. Credit: Vermont.com Mad River Glen has finalized the purchase of more than 1,100 acres of land surrounding its ski area in Fayston, Vermont, securing the property for conservation and backcountry recreation. The cooperatively owned ski area closed the $2.5 million deal Tuesday, general manager Matt Lillard told VTDigger . The acquisition more than doubles the land owned by the resort’s cooperative, which previously held about 700 acres. Lillard said the property will not be developed and instead will be protected for recreational and conservation purposes. “We will be putting the land in conservation with a focus on recreation and backcountry skiing. We’re not looking to develop it in any way,” he told VTDigger . The purchase was financed through a crowdsourced fundraising effort that raised roughly $2.8 million, according to the resort

articles.title: Abandoned Ski Resort in Michigan Purchased For $8 Million By Conservancy Group
articles.description: The Leelanau Conservancy completed the acquisition of the former Sugar Loaf Ski Resort following an $8 million fundraising campaign.
articles.content: Cleveland Township, Mich. — An abandoned Northern Michigan ski resort is set to become a public outdoor recreation area after a land conservancy finalized the purchase of the property. MLive reports The Leelanau Conservancy announced March 11 that it completed the acquisition of the former Sugar Loaf Ski Resort following an $8 million fundraising campaign. The 288-acre property will now undergo ecological restoration and be developed for public access. History of Sugar Loaf Ski Resort Plans for the site include a network of hiking and biking trails along with winter recreation such as snowshoeing, cross-country skiing and backcountry skiing. Officials also plan to add universal-access features, a family sledding hill, a nature play area and a summit pavilion overlooking Lake Michigan. Conservation efforts will focus on removing invasive species, reducing erosion and improving local water quality. The Edmund F. and Virginia B. Ball Foundation purchased the property in 2020 through a sub

articles.title: Swiss Ski Resorts Are Changing
articles.description: Ski resorts and winter sports destinations are re-thinking what they offer. That’s according to a report on SwissInfo. NEW The post Swiss Ski Resorts Are Changing appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: The publication lists five main changes underway. See here for the full article on SwissInfo , and we summarise it below. Ski resorts are changing ownership Swiss ski resorts are mainly owned by cable car and lift companies, in which local communities often hold a stake but do not have majority control. In autumn 2025, the municipalities of Flims, Laax and Falera, in the canton of Graubünden, made headlines when they banded together to buy their ski area, “Weisse Arena”. Vail Resorts from the United States, the world’s largest ski resort operator, has set its sights on the Alps. It has already acquired the ski lifts of Crans-Montana and Andermatt-Sedrun. Vail Resorts Confirms it is looking to buy more resorts in the Alps Crans Montana – photo c/o Snow Camp The company often focuses on sites with development potential where lifts, accommodation and leisure facilities can be combined into one huge resort. The presence of these new investors is changing the character of the mountain regio

articles.title: New Zealand’s Largest Ski Resort Details Offseason Projects
articles.description: After opening a new terrain expansion last year, the New Zealand ski resort is making various improvements across the mountain.
articles.content: New Zealand — Cardrona Alpine Resort made a lot of major moves last year. The opening of the Soho Basin expansion made Cardrona the largest ski resort in New Zealand. While they haven’t been as busy as last offseason, work continues on various improvements. Cardrona has given guests a few updates regarding projects this offseason. Regarding Soho Basin, redesigned runs and a snowmaking basin will be added. This will allow for more reliable conditions and earlier openings. In terms of redesigned runs, one example is Snow Farmer. Last winter, guests used the summer road to ski down, which wasn’t the route’s intended purpose. This upcoming winter will see a defined piste, meaning it’s easier to get down on it. Last month, Cardrona showed work on various projects. Work continues on a new snowmaking dam/pond, which will greatly increase their snowmaking capacity. A new pumphouse is being added, which will allow for snowmaking in Soho Basin. Third, upgrades are being made to the base area. Th

articles.title: California Ski Resort Won’t Reopen This Season
articles.description: A California ski resort closed again last week due to low snowpack, and the long-range forecast has led to the end of its season.
articles.content: McCloud, California — Earlier this week, it appeared that the writing could be on the wall for Mt. Shasta Ski Park. Early last week, Mt. Shasta Ski Park announced it was temporarily shutting down operations. On Sunday , I questioned whether Mt. Shasta Ski Park would be able to reopen due to a disappearing snowpack at the base area and a dry forecast in the days ahead. It turns out that those concerns were justified. On Tuesday, Mt. Shasta Ski Park announced that its 2025-26 ski season has come to a close. A less-than-ideal forecast, along with the continued depreciation of the snowpack, led the ski resort to throw in the towel. View this post on Instagram There were some good moments this season at the Mt. Shasta Ski Park. Major dumps in late December and February have led to some nice powder skiing. Unfortunately, warm weather and rain have also played a role, which resulted in two closures. The first came on February 9th, but didn’t last too long due to the new snow. The second and u

articles.title: Indy Pass Adds 16 New Ski Areas
articles.description: Indy Pass has announced the addition of sixteen new ski areas for the 2026/27 season. Six are in Europe and there are more to come. NEW The post Indy Pass Adds 16 New Ski Areas appeared first on PlanetSKI .
articles.content: Indy Pass is announcing the addition of sixteen new ski areas for the 26/27 season. Here at PlanetSKI we have had an Indy Pass for the past two seasons and find it gives us access to some small gems of resorts that are off the radar of many skiers, but well worth a visit. Europe: Les 7 Laux, France Bergbahnen Hohsaas AG, Switzerland Valmorel, France Stöten i Sälen, Sweden Levi, Finland Stranda Ski Resort, Norway North America: Murray Ski Ridge, Canada Pebble Creek Ski Area, USA Thrill Hills, USA Bethel Village Trails XC, USA Franconia Inn & Outdoor Center XC, USA Great Glen Trails Outdoor Center XC, USA Northern Maine Community Trails XC, USA Steamboat Ski Touring Center XC, USA Haymaker Nordic Center XC, USA The Loppet XC, USA The Indy Pass will continue to add resorts over the off-season and expects major additions before the 26/27 season. “Indy Pass has experienced remarkable growth and with that growth comes responsibility,” said Indy Pass Director, Erik Mogensen. “We must continue
